title: DSDS Pattern Definitions
description: Defines documents for interaction patterns. Patterns are repeated solutions to common UX problems: navigation, error messaging, form validation, empty states, loading sequences, and onboarding flows. A pattern document holds the identity of a multi-component solution. Typed entries in the `metadata` array carry modular metadata: status, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Typed entries in the `documentBlock` array carry structured docs.

pattern.description: A repeated, multi-component solution to a common UX problem. Patterns sit above individual components and show how those components compose to meet a user need. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag — status, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlock` array holds structured docs as typed entries: `interaction` for flow steps, `guideline` for rules, `accessibility` for a11y specs, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a pattern.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the pattern (e.g., 'error-messaging', 'navigation', 'empty-state', 'form-validation'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Error Messaging', 'Navigation', 'Empty State').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this pattern, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Patterns accept the pattern-specific kinds (interactions) plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Interaction entries rely on order to show the flow in time. Reference the components a pattern uses through a `links` metadata entry (type 'component', name, and role) rather than a dedicated block kind.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this pattern: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.
